reasoning: The stock market generally exhibits a slight positive drift, but DHI is currently facing strong negative momentum. The company reported a 25% YoY decline in net income and weak FY2026 guidance on Oct 28, 2025, and a class-action lawsuit from homebuyers was filed in early November 2025. Per the post-earnings announcement drift (PEAD) phenomenon, stocks with negative guidance/earnings surprises often experience an extended period of downward drift for 30-60 days. Therefore, over the 1-week and 1-month horizons, it is slightly more likely that the downward pressure will continue, keeping the expected chance of an increase relative to the Nov 9 baseline below 50%. By the 3-month horizon, the effects of the initial earnings shock will have largely faded, offset by consistent share repurchases and preparations for the spring selling season. Taking into account the instruction that longer horizons must have probabilities closer to 0.5 (representing maximum uncertainty without relying over-confidently on positive market drift), the 3-month forecast is 0.50.
